rationale: June payrolls came in significantly weaker than expected with downward revisions — a fresh catalyst reducing near-term Fed hike probability that is not yet fully absorbed in gold prices after a 12.7% three-month correction. The Fed's June FOMC minutes due Wednesday (Jul 9) are the key swing event: a dovish internal tone consistent with the labor-market softening could catalyze a bounce from deeply oversold levels toward $4,300 resistance. Confidence is held low because nominal yields at 4.49%, a firmer DXY, and the ~50% year-end hike probability priced by markets remain structural headwinds th
key_drivers: weak June payrolls reducing near-term Fed hike bets
Fed June minutes (Jul 9) — dovish risk asymmetry
DXY direction — recent firmness capping bounce
real yields at ~4.49% nominal structurally bearish
